mapper.sql: u.id, u.username, u.real_name, u.email, u.phone, u.department_id, u.status, u.password_hash, u.version
mapper.select: select u.id, u.username, u.real_name, u.email, u.phone, u.department_id, u.status, u.password_hash, u.version from sys_user u where u.id = #{id}
select u.id, u.username, u.real_name, u.email, u.phone, u.department_id, u.status, u.password_hash, u.version from sys_user u where u.username = #{username}
select u.id, u.username, u.real_name, u.email, u.phone, u.department_id, u.status, u.password_hash, u.version from sys_user u where u.email = #{email}
mapper.update: update sys_user set username = #{username}, real_name = #{realName}, email = #{email}, phone = #{phone}, department_id = #{departmentId}, status = #{status}, password_hash = #{passwordHash}, version = version + 1 where id = #{id}
mapper.select: select role_id from sys_user_role where user_id = #{userId}

select user_id as userId, role_id as roleId from sys_user_role where user_id in
#{userId}
select r.code from sys_user_role ur join sys_role r on ur.role_id = r.id where ur.user_id = #{userId}
select r.name from sys_user_role ur join sys_role r on ur.role_id = r.id where ur.user_id = #{userId} order by r.name asc

select distinct m.code from sys_user_role ur join sys_role r on ur.role_id = r.id join sys_role_menu rm on rm.role_id = r.id join sys_menu m on m.id = rm.menu_id where ur.user_id = #{userId} and r.status in ('enabled', 'active') and m.status in ('enabled', 'active') and m.code is not null and m.code != ''
select r.data_scope from sys_user_role ur join sys_role r on r.id = ur.role_id where ur.user_id = #{userId} and r.status in ('enabled', 'active')

select distinct rd.dept_id from sys_user_role ur join sys_role r on r.id = ur.role_id join sys_role_dept rd on rd.role_id = r.id where ur.user_id = #{userId} and r.status in ('enabled', 'active') and r.data_scope = 'custom'
select id, password_hash as passwordHash from sys_user
